title: Fed holds interest rates steady for third straight meeting, as Powell vows to remain as governor
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Federal Reserve held interest rates steady for the third consecutive meeting and Chair Jerome Powell announced he will remain as a board governor after his

content.introduction: The Federal Reserve maintained its benchmark interest rates unchanged for the third consecutive meeting, signaling a cautious approach to monetary policy amid persistent inflation concerns. Concurrently, Chair Jerome Powell announced his decision to remain on the Federal Reserve Board as a governor after his term as chair expires, citing the importance of preserving the institution's independence amidst growing political challenges.
body:
## Fed Maintains Current Policy Stance

The Federal Reserve's Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) voted unanimously to keep the target range for the federal funds rate between 5.25% and 5.50%, marking the third consecutive meeting without a rate adjustment. This decision reflects the central bank's ongoing assessment of inflation progress toward its 2% target, which remains above the desired pace despite recent moderation. In the post-meeting statement, the committee acknowledged that 'economic activity has been expanding at a solid pace,' while noting that 'inflation has eased but remains elevated.' The Fed's updated dot plot revealed that officials project three rate cuts in 2024, down from the four cuts anticipated in December, indicating a more cautious approach to future policy adjustments. Market participants have now priced in a 62% probability of a rate cut in June, according to the CME FedWatch Tool, reflecting the delicate balancing act the Fed faces between taming inflation and supporting economic growth.

## Powell's Decision to Remain as Governor

Chair Jerome Powell surprised financial markets by announcing he will accept a position as a board governor after his current term as Fed chair concludes in February 2026. This marks the first time since 1948 that a sitting Fed chair has chosen to remain on the board rather than leaving the institution entirely. Powell explained his decision was motivated by concerns regarding the Fed's independence, stating in a press conference that 'it's very important that we maintain the independence of the Fed from short-term political pressures.' His announcement comes amid increasing political rhetoric questioning the Fed's operations, particularly from some Republican lawmakers who have criticized the central bank's monetary policy decisions. Powell's decision ensures continuity in monetary policy implementation and provides stability during a period of potential political turbulence. The move also allows Powell to continue shaping the Fed's institutional framework and monetary strategy beyond his chairmanship, potentially influencing the central bank's approach for years to come.

## Market Implications and Analyst Perspectives

Financial markets responded to the Fed's decision with tempered optimism, as the S&P 500 gained 0.4% following the announcement, while Treasury yields remained relatively stable. Analysts at Goldman Sachs commented that the Fed's 'higher for longer' message was already priced into markets, explaining the muted reaction. However, some economists expressed concern that the Fed's revised projections may indicate a more prolonged period of restrictive monetary policy than previously anticipated. 'The dot plot shift suggests the Fed is becoming more data-dependent rather than calendar-dependent,' noted Michael Feroli, chief U.S. economist at JPMorgan Chase. 'This means the timing of any rate cuts will depend heavily on incoming inflation data rather than a predetermined schedule.' Meanwhile, bond markets have recalibrated expectations, with the 10-year Treasury yield rising to 4.30% in the days following the meeting, reflecting investors' reassessment of the Fed's policy trajectory. The decision by Powell to remain as governor has been viewed favorably by institutional investors, who value the predictability and experience he brings to the central bank's decision-making process.

content.outlook: Looking ahead, Fed Chair Jerome Powell said the Iran conflict could be an inflationary factor in the short term but noted its long-term impact remains uncertain. Powell also announced he would remain on the Fed's Board of Governors until the Justice Department's investigation concludes, creating a new variable in . Market participants will continue to monitor developments closely for signals about the direction of fed policy trends and their broader implications for portfolio allocation and risk management strategies.
